content: The open-ended exploratory drive of curiosity-driven reinforcement learning (ICM/RND frameworks) can be reframed as a built-in active memory pruning mechanism for generalist agents, where prediction error (the core curiosity signal) doubles as a metric to prioritize storing only high-uncertainty, non-redundant experience that improves future exploration. This replaces the conventional view of curiosity as only a reward signal for exploration and memory as a separate passive storage component.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
